description: Paired 200-Agent sensitivity for the 60+ ride-hailing mode constant only.
scope_notes: Only the 60+ ride-hailing age-mode constant changes.
Digital access, family assistance, activities, destinations, fares, waiting, weather behavior and the 36-vehicle fleet remain unchanged.
The experiment screens behavioral plausibility and does not calibrate a real-world elderly ride-hailing share.
The A4 upper sensitivity equals the existing 18-39 ride-hailing constant; it is a utility constant, not a 30-percent choice probability.
The five evenly spaced values are used to identify a smooth response or a behavioral threshold without forcing an elderly mode share.
